title: Marty Supreme Secures Nine Oscar Nominations for 2026
text: The A24 film "Marty Supreme" earned nine Academy Award nominations, including Best Picture. Director Josh Safdie's debut Oscar nomination includes directing, original screenplay, and film editing categories. Screenplay Recognition: Safdie collaborated with Ronald Bronstein on the original screenplay nomination. The pair also received recognition for film editing. According to the article, they "received nods for writing the screenplay for 'Marty Supreme,'" competing against films like "Blue Moon", "It Was Just an Accident", "Sentimental Value", and "Sinners". Leading Actor Makes History: Star Timothée Chalamet secured his third acting nomination at age 30, becoming "the youngest male actor to score three acting nominations since Marlon Brando in 1954." His previous nominations came from "Call Me by Your Name" (2018) and "A Complete Unknown" (2025). Additional Recognition: The film received nominations across multiple technical categories: cinematography (Darius Khondji), costume design (Miyako Bellizzi), production design (Jack Fisk), and the newly established Best Casting category (Jennifer Venditti).
